Looking forward to catching up again soon.

Lucas

conversations.content: Can you explain what tonsillar remnants are after a childhood tonsillectomy, even when there are no complications?

Certainly! Tonsillar remnants refer to the small amounts of tonsil tissue that might be left behind after a tonsillectomy. Even when a tonsillectomy is performed perfectly and the patient experiences no recurring health problems, it is possible that some residual tissue remains. This small amount of tissue is generally not a cause for concern if it does not lead to any further infections or issues.
